commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can harden the suffix allow-list and tests without choosing a new product direction.; Needs attention: The diff improves several leak cases but still introduces a concrete suffix-leak path for missed multi-word and Title-Case diagnostic headers or IDs.; Review finding: [P1] Reject remaining diagnostic key/value suffixes — src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/sanitize-user-facing-text.ts... (sha=8c4fd2964db7623f26083a2f0282df6861286d63)
commands.review_summary:
The branch changes `sanitizeUserFacingText` to classify a leading error line, append allow-listed follow-up prose, reject stack/JSON/HTML/diagnostic suffixes, and add sanitizer regression tests.

PR surface: Source +107, Tests +357. Total +464 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the code path: current main routes error-stop assistant text through `sanitizeUserFacingText` with `errorContext: true`, and the PR body includes terminal probes plus inspected Discord before/after screenshots. I did not run the live Discord scenario myself in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can harden the suffix allow-list and tests without choosing a new product direction.
